rows.notes: Page title clearly centered at top: 'Soluta Ordinaria' with date 'Novr. 2. 1815'. Very legible.
Description reads clearly as 'Curat. Pson.'. Amount '4' sits in the pounds column (between first and second ruling lines) with no shillings/pence marks; column alignment unambiguous.

Description interpreted as 'Mayor; Captn.' The numeric group adjacent aligns with the shillings and pence columns: '14' in shillings column and '8' in pence column. Handwriting of pence slightly faint so chosen reading 8d based on alignment and visible loop.
Description uncertain ('Curia Writings' is an interpretation of cursive). Nearby amount reads as '1' in pounds column; shillings and pence fields blank. Lettering of description slightly ambiguous.

Description very uncertain (transcribed literally from script). Amount shows '10' in shillings column and a small '1' in pence column; alignment of the small pence digit is faint so confidence moderate.
Description read as 'Dr. Sub Treasurer' from the script. Amount '4.12.6' is clearly written and aligns well with the three narrow columns; digits are distinct and valid.
Description uncertain (reading of name is ambiguous). Amount appears as '12.6' aligned in the shilling/pence columns; pounds column is blank. Ink a little light on the description.
Short description (partly illegible). Amount '6.2' visible in shillings/pence columns; digits legible but description uncertain.
Handwritten description partially illegible; interpreted as 'Goth L. & Bapt.' Amount appears to be '2s 10d' (shillings and pence) but the pence digit is somewhat stylized; assigned low confidence.
Description partially inferred ('Preparations' reads clearly; name is less clear). Amount '2.10.9' aligns with columns but ink slightly faded on pence; chosen reading 9d.
Description very uncertain; reading from script. Amount looks like '10' in pounds column but alignment with ruling lines is not perfect; low confidence.
Short, faded description (uncertain). Amount appears as '1.14.0' with the '1' in pounds and '14' in shillings, but digits slightly smudged.
Latin-like description partially legible. Amount '5.5.0' is visible and aligns with the three columns; description letters are faint but readable.
'do' indicates repetition of previous heading; remainder of description is partially faded. Amount '5.5.0' visible again; confidence moderate due to ink fade.
Name 'Mitchell' and 'Pension' readable. Amount '5.5.0' clearly written and aligned.
Another 'do' row mirroring previous. Amount repeats as '5.5.0' but the trailing words are faded; moderate confidence.
Description partly Latin and faded; amount '2.2.0' is visible and neatly aligned in shillings/pence columns (pounds column shows 2).
Continuation 'do' line. Amount '2.2.0' repeats; ink slightly lighter than previous line.
Description 'Vicar of Morton' readable, with a name/abbrev 'L. Day'. Amount appears as '15s' in shillings column (no pounds); the '15' is distinct.
Second line relating to Vicar with similar amount '15s'. Wording after dash is unclear; amount reading relies on repetition of digit shapes; lower confidence due to faded ink.

The number adjacent looks like '40' but this would be 40 shillings (which is unusual as shillings column should be 0–19). This likely indicates misalignment or that '40' is actually '2' pounds (notated as 40s). Because of column ambiguity this row has low confidence—kept as shillings per direct reading but flagged in notes.
Description partly illegible. Adjacent figures read as '1.10.0' but letters are faint and some ink bleed; moderate to low confidence.
'Postage' is readable. Amount seems to be '2.10.3' aligned across the three columns; the final pence digit is small and slightly smudged, chosen as '3d'.

rows.description: Land Tax St. Michael for Coffins House
rows.notes: Description legible enough to read 'Land Tax St. Michael for Coffins House'. Amount appears as '1.7.6' and is clear in the columns.
Short description 'House & Window' readable; the adjacent amount is faint and looks like '2s' in the shillings column with no pounds/pence visible. Low confidence due to faintness.

Description uncertain ('University Marshal' inferred). The drawn figures look like '2.12.?' but the '12' is ambiguous: if that were pence it would exceed 11 — indicating likely misalignment. Because of column uncertainty, recorded as shillings=2, pence=12 (but pence of 12 is invalid); due to validation rule, this reading is flagged with low confidence. (Supervisor: likely misaligned; re-check column boundaries recommended.)
Description 'Crown Prices' legible. Amount '12s' visible in shillings column; pounds column blank. Ink on number clear but context slightly ambiguous.
Description reads 'Watch-man'. Amount '12s' appears in shillings column; the handwriting on the number is reasonably clear.
Part of description legible: 'Syndals & Procurations Kilmington'. Amount '1.4.10' can be read across the three columns; letters and digits reasonably distinct.
Description 'House & Window ad & Taxes' is plausible reading. Amount '1.6.9' sits clearly in the three currency columns.
Description partly legible; 'Clerk' and date fragment '6th die Dec.' visible. Amount shows '4s' but is faint and could be misaligned; low confidence.
Final total '153.18.2' is clearly written at right near the lower section of the main list and aligns exactly with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling columns; high confidence.
Small separate section marker on lower left, beginning a pensions subsection. The '#' and word 'pensions' readable; marker indicates a new group header.
Handwritten note in lower-left block. Reads 'Danffle. Do 3 yrs' (name partly uncertain). Amount '1.12.0' clearly written to the right of this line in the small block.
Second pension line in the lower block. Name is partly abbreviated; amount '1.6.9' reads clearly and matches visible digits.
Subtotal of the small pensions block handwritten as '2.10.9'. Large clear digits and obvious addition of the two preceding amounts; alignment in small block is clear.
Initials 'I.C.S.' (or similar) clearly written beneath the small block. No amount associated; treated as an annotation/initials line.
